Name: Bulbasaur
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: ﻿For some time after its birth, it uses the nutrients that are packed into the seed on its back in order to grow.

Name: Ivysaur
Flavor-Text: The more sunlight Ivysaur bathes in, the more strength wells up within it, allowing the bud on its back to grow larger.

Name: Mega Venusaur ex
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may use this Ability. Move a Basic [G] Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Exeggcute
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic [G] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: If you touch one of Exeggcute’s heads, mistaking it for an egg, the other heads will quickly gather and attack you in a swarm.

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
This attack does 30 more damage for each [G] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is called the Walking Jungle. Each of the nuts has a face and a will of its own.

Name: Tangela
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.

Name: Tangrowth
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack’s cost), this attack does 140 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Vine growth is accelerated for Tangrowth living in warm climates. If the vines grow long, Tangrowth shortens them by tearing parts of them off.

Name: Chikorita
Flavor-Text: It loves to bask in the sunlight. It uses the leaf on its head to seek out warm places.

Name: Bayleef
Effects: Switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The spicy scent that wafts from around Bayleef’s neck somehow makes those who smell it want to fight.

Name: Meganium
Ability-Effect: Each Basic [G] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon provides [G][G] Energy. The effect of Wild Growth doesn’t stack.
Flavor-Text: Anyone who stands beside it becomes refreshed, just as if they were soaking in the soothing atmosphere of a forest.

Name: Shuckle
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon has any [G] Energy attached, you may use this Ability. Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The berries stored in its vaselike shell eventually become a thick, pulpy juice.

Name: Celebi
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 in any combination of [G] Pokémon and Stadium cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon traveled through time to come from the future. It bolsters grass and trees with its own strength, and it can heal wounds, too.

Name: Seedot
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: To expand its habitat, it pretends to be a nut so that bird Pokémon will pick it up and carry it far away.

Name: Nuzleaf
Flavor-Text: It leads a quiet life deep in the forest. If anything wanders into its territory, Nuzleaf will warn the intruder by sounding its grass flute.

Name: Shiftry
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. Shuffle that Pokémon and all attached cards into their deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It is said that when Shiftry flaps its leafy fans atop ancient trees, chilly winds blow and the season rolls into winter.

Name: Nincada
Flavor-Text: It can sometimes live underground for more than 10 years. It absorbs nutrients from the roots of trees.

Name: Ninjask
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for a Shedinja and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is so quick, it is said to be able to avoid any attack. It loves to feed on tree sap.

Name: Dhelmise
Effects: If you have a Stadium in play, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: After lowering its anchor, it waits for its prey. It catches large Wailord and drains their life-force.

Name: Vulpix
Flavor-Text: If it is attacked by an enemy that is stronger than itself, it feigns injury to fool the enemy and escapes.

Name: Ninetales
Effects: Discard the top card of your deck, and if that card is a Supporter card, use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack.
Flavor-Text: Some legends claim that each of its nine tails has its own unique type of special mystical power.

Name: Numel
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Magma of almost 2,200 degrees Fahrenheit courses through its body. When it grows cold, the magma hardens and slows it.

Name: Mega Camerupt ex
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Burned, this attack does 160 more damage.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Litleo
Flavor-Text: When Litleo are young, female Pyroar will teach them how to hunt. Once the Litleo mature, they will leave the pride and set out on their own.

Name: Pyroar
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent’s Active Pokémon do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: The mane of a male Pyroar heats up to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit during battle. Merely approaching it will cause severe burns.

Name: Volcanion
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Put 2 [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.

Name: Scorbunny
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Once its body has heated up, Scorbunny can use the full extent of its power. That’s why it does warm-up exercises.

Name: Raboot
Effects: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Thanks to its soft and fluffy fur, Raboot can easily heat up its fire energy and produce flames with more power.

Name: Cinderace
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in your hand when you are setting up to play, you may put it face down in the Active Spot.
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Although Cinderace loses its cool easily, it will battle flawlessly for a Trainer it trusts.

Name: Sizzlipede
Flavor-Text: It wraps prey up with its heated body, cooking them in its coils. Once they’re well-done, it will voraciously nibble them down to the last morsel.

Name: Centiskorch
Effects: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: While its burning body is already dangerous on its own, this excessively hostile Pokémon also has large and very sharp fangs.

Name: Chi-Yu
Effects: If your opponent has a Stadium in play, discard it. If you do, your opponent can’t play any Stadium cards from their hand during their next turn.
Flavor-Text: It controls flames burning at over 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit. It casually swims through the sea of lava it creates by melting rock and sand.

Name: Mantine
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: As it majestically swims, it doesn’t care if Remoraid attach to it to scavenge for its leftovers.

Name: Corphish
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It came from overseas. It is a very hardy creature that will quickly proliferate, even in polluted streams.

Name: Kyogre
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Basic [W] Energy card in your discard pile. Then, shuffle those cards into your deck.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said to have widened the seas by causing downpours. It had been asleep in a marine trench.

Name: Snover
Flavor-Text: During cold seasons, it migrates to the mountain’s lower reaches. It returns to the snow-covered summit in the spring.

Name: Mega Abomasnow ex
Effects: Discard the top 6 cards of your deck, and this attack does 100 damage for each Basic [W] Energy card that you discarded in this way.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Clauncher
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon launches water by detonating gas inside its right claw. It snipes flying Pokémon.

Name: Clawitzer
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from the Active Spot to your Bench, you may use this Ability. Attach up to 2 Basic [W] Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Clawitzer can move through the water at a speed of 60 knots by using gas inside its body to expel water from the back of its claw.

Name: Sobble
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It gets berries to eat by shooting them down with bullets of water it spurts from its mouth. Its aim is perfect.

Name: Drizzile
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It extends its long, slimy tongue at blinding speeds and finishes off its prey with great skill.

Name: Inteleon
Effects: Choose a Pokémon in play (yours or your opponent’s) that has the least HP remaining, except for this Pokémon, and it is Knocked Out.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses special lenses in its eyes to sense things about its opponents—like their body heat—then attacks once it identifies a weak spot.

Name: Snom
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It eats snow that has accumulated on the ground. It prefers soft, freshly fallen snow, so it will eat its way up a mountain, aiming for the peak.

Name: Frosmoth
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Frosmoth senses air currents with its antennae. It sends its scales drifting on frigid air, making them fall like snow.

Name: Eiscue
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: On hot days, these Pokémon press their ice cube heads together and pass the time cooling each other down.

Name: Magnemite
Flavor-Text: The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

Name: Magneton
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Three Magnemite are linked by a strong magnetic force. Earaches will occur if you get too close.

Name: Magnezone
Effects: If this Pokémon evolved from Magneton during this turn, this attack does 120 more damage.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Flashing Bolt.
Flavor-Text: As it zooms through the sky, this Pokémon seems to be receiving signals of unknown origin while transmitting signals of unknown purpose.

Name: Raikou
Effects: If you have at least 4 [L] Energy in play, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This rough Pokémon stores energy inside its body, then sweeps across the land, shooting off electricity.

Name: Electrike
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It stores electricity in its fur. It gives off sparks from all over its body in seasons when the air is dry.

Name: Mega Manectric ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.
You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon and have this attack do 130 more damage.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Pachirisu
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, whenever they attach an Energy card from their hand to the Defending Pokémon, place 8 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s one of the kinds of Pokémon with electric cheek pouches. It shoots charges from its tail.

Name: Helioptile
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The sun powers this Pokémon’s electricity generation. Interruption of that process stresses Helioptile to the point of weakness.

Name: Heliolisk
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: One Heliolisk basking in the sun with its frill outspread is all it would take to produce enough electricity to power a city.

Name: Abra
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Abra can teleport in its sleep. Apparently the more deeply Abra sleeps, the farther its teleportations go.

Name: Kadabra
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s telekinesis is immensely powerful. To prepare for evolution, Kadabra stores up psychic energy in the star on its forehead.

Name: Alakazam
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Draw 3 cards.
Effects: Place 2 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon for each card in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Alakazam wields potent psychic powers. It’s said that this Pokémon used these powers to create the spoons it holds.

Name: Jynx
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its strange cries sound like human language. There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.

Name: Ralts
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people’s emotions.

Name: Kirlia
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

Name: Mega Gardevoir ex
Effects: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a Basic [P] Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 50 damage for each [P] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Shedinja
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon ex, your opponent can’t take any Prize cards for it.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A strange Pokémon–it flies without moving its wings, has a hollow shell for a body, and does not breathe.

Name: Spoink
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Spoink will die if it stops bouncing. The pearl on its head amplifies its psychic powers.

Name: Grumpig
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and attach any number of Basic Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: It can perform odd dance steps to influence foes. Its style of dancing became hugely popular overseas.

Name: Xerneas
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic [P] Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Bright Horns.
Flavor-Text: When the horns on its head shine in seven colors, it is said to be sharing everlasting life.

Name: Greavard
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It is said that a dog Pokémon that died in the wild without ever interacting with a human was reborn as this Pokémon.

Name: Houndstone
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into their deck.
Flavor-Text: Houndstone spends most of its time sleeping in graveyards. Among all the dog Pokémon, this one is most loyal to its master.

Name: Gimmighoul
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was born inside a treasure chest about 1,500 years ago. It sucks the life-force out of scoundrels who try to steal the treasure.

Name: Sandshrew
Flavor-Text: It digs deep burrows to live in. When in danger, it rolls up its body to withstand attacks.

Name: Sandslash
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn’t happen.
Flavor-Text: It is adept at attacking with the spines on its back and its sharp claws while quickly scurrying about.

Name: Onix
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It rapidly bores through the ground at 50 mph by squirming and twisting its massive, rugged body.

Name: Tyrogue
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This earnest Pokémon battles all sorts of opponents, searching for the fighting style that suits it best.

Name: Makuhita
Flavor-Text: It grows stronger by enduring harsh training. It is a gutsy Pokémon that can withstand any attack.

Name: Hariyama
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 70 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It loves challenging others to tests of strength. It has the power to stop a train with a slap.

Name: Lunatone
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if you have Solrock in play, you may discard a Basic [F] Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Draw 3 cards. You can’t use more than 1 Lunar Cycle Ability each turn.
Flavor-Text: It was discovered at the site of a meteor strike 40 years ago. Its stare can lull its foes to sleep.

Name: Solrock
Effects: If you don’t have Lunatone on your Bench, this attack does nothing. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Solar energy is the source of its power, so it is strong during the daytime. When it spins, its body shines.

Name: Riolu
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Accelerating Stab.
Flavor-Text: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

Name: Mega Lucario ex
Effects: Attach up to 3 Basic [F] Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Mega Brave.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Croagunk
Flavor-Text: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

Name: Toxicroak
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Swaying and dodging the attacks of its foes, it weaves its flexible body in close, then lunges out with its poisonous claws.

Name: Marshadow
Effects: If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can conceal itself in any shadow, so it went undiscovered for a long time.

Name: Stonjourner
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use attacks.
Flavor-Text: The elemental composition of the rocks that form its body were found to match the bedrock of a land far away from this Pokémon’s habitat.

Name: Nacli
Flavor-Text: It was born in a layer of rock salt deep under the earth. This species was particularly treasured in the old days, as they would share precious salt.

Name: Naclstack
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon dry cures its prey by spraying salt over them. The curing process steals away the water in the prey’s body.

Name: Garganacl
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by your [F] Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Garganacl will rub its fingertips together and sprinkle injured Pokémon with salt. Even severe wounds will promptly heal afterward.

Name: Crawdaunt
Effects: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack can be used for [D].
Flavor-Text: Loving to battle, this Pokémon pinches all Pokémon that enter its territory with its pincers and throws them out.

Name: Mega Absol ex
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has exactly 6 damage counters on it, that Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Your opponent reveals their hand, and you discard a card you find there.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Spiritomb
Ability-Effect: If your Active [D] Pokémon is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if your Active [D] Pokémon is Knocked Out), place 1 damage counter on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.

Name: Yveltal
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

Name: Nickit
Flavor-Text: Cunning and cautious, this Pokémon survives by stealing food from others. It erases its tracks with swipes of its tail as it makes off with its plunder.

Name: Thievul
Effects: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: With a lithe body and sharp claws, it goes around stealing food and eggs. Boltund is its natural enemy.

Name: Shroodle
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Though usually a mellow Pokémon, it will sink its sharp, poison-soaked front teeth into any that anger it, causing paralysis in the object of its ire.

Name: Grafaiai
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a Special Condition. Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: The color of the poisonous saliva depends on what the Pokémon eats. Grafaiai covers its fingers in its saliva and draws patterns on trees in forests.

Name: Steelix
Effects: If you have exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, this attack does 200 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It is thought its body transformed as a result of iron accumulating internally from swallowing soil.

Name: Mega Mawile ex
Effects: This attack does 80 damage for each Prize card you have taken.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack’s base damage is 30.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Dialga
Effects: You may shuffle all Energy attached to this Pokémon into your deck and have this attack do 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has the power to control time. It appears in Sinnoh-region myths as an ancient deity.

Name: Tinkatink
Flavor-Text: It swings its handmade hammer around to protect itself, but the hammer is often stolen by Pokémon that eat metal.

Name: Tinkatuff
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon will attack groups of Pawniard and Bisharp, gathering metal from them in order to create a large and sturdy hammer.

Name: Tinkaton
Effects: This attack does 60 less damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This intelligent Pokémon has a very daring disposition. It knocks rocks into the sky with its hammer, aiming for flying Corviknight.

Name: Gholdengo
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. Search your deck for a number of cards up to the number of heads and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its body seems to be made up of 1,000 coins. This Pokémon gets along well with others and is quick to make friends with anybody.

Name: Mega Latias ex
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Latios
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when your Mega Latias ex moves from your Bench to the Active Spot, you may use this Ability. Move any amount of Energy from your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It understands human speech and is highly intelligent. It is a tender Pokémon that dislikes fighting.

Name: Spearow
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Due to its short wings, it can’t fly long distances. It wanders about restlessly and pecks at bug Pokémon.

Name: Fearow
Effects: Flip 5 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Carrying food through Fearow’s territory is dangerous. It will snatch the food away from you in a flash!

Name: Mega Kangaskhan ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may use this Ability. Draw 2 cards. You can’t use more than 1 Run Errand Ability each turn.
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Delibird
Effects: If you go first, you can use this attack during your first turn. Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It carries food all day long. There are tales about lost people who were saved by the food it had.

Name: Miltank
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon needs to be milked every day, or else it will fall ill. The flavor of Miltank milk changes with the seasons.

Name: Buneary
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Buneary can attack by rolling up their ears and then striking with the force created by unrolling them. This attack becomes stronger with training.

Name: Lopunny
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Once hot seasons are over, Lopunny’s coat will be replaced with fur that holds a lot of insulating air in preparation for colder weather.

Name: Yungoos
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Its stomach fills most of its torso. It wanders the same path every day, searching for fresh food.

Name: Gumshoos
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Switch a card from your hand with the top card of your deck.
Flavor-Text: Once it finds signs of prey, it will patiently stake out the location, waiting until the sun goes down.

Name: Stufful
Flavor-Text: The way it protects itself by flailing its arms may be an adorable sight, but stay well away. This is flailing that can snap thick tree trunks.

Name: Bewear
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The moves it uses to take down its prey would make a martial artist jealous. It tucks subdued prey under its arms to carry them to its nest.

Name: Acerola’s Mischief
Description: You can use this card only if your opponent has 2 or fewer Prize cards remaining.Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play. During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to that Pokémon by your opponent’s Pokémon ex.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Boss’s Orders
Description: Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Energy Switch
Description: Move a Basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Fighting Gong
Description: Search your deck for a Basic [F] Energy card or a Basic [F] Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Forest of Vitality
Description: Each player’s [G] Pokémon can evolve into [G] Pokémon during the turn they play those Pokémon, except during their first turn.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Iron Defender
Description: During your opponent’s next turn, all of your [M] Pokémon take 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (This includes new Pokémon that come into play.)
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Lillie’s Determination
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards. If you have exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, draw 8 cards instead.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Lt. Surge’s Bargain
Description: Ask your opponent if each player may take a Prize card. If yes, each player takes a Prize card. If no, you draw 4 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Mega Signal
Description: Search your deck for a Mega Evolution Pokémon ex, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Mystery Garden
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may discard an Energy card from their hand in order to draw cards until they have as many cards in their hand as they have [P] Pokémon in play.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Pokémon Center Lady
Description: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon, and it recovers from all Special Conditions.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Premium Power Pro
Description: During this turn, attacks used by your [F] Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Rare Candy
Description: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can’t use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Repel
Description: Switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Risky Ruins
Description: Whenever any player puts a Basic non-[D] Pokémon onto their Bench during their turn, place 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Strange Timepiece
Description: Devolve 1 of your evolved [P] Pokémon by putting any number of Evolution cards on it into your hand. (That Pokémon can’t evolve this turn.)
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Surfing Beach
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may switch their Active [W] Pokémon with 1 of their Benched [W] Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Switch
Description: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Ultra Ball
Description: You can use this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand.Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Wally’s Compassion
Description: Heal all damage from 1 of your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex. If you healed any damage in this way, put all Energy attached to that Pokémon into your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Bulbasaur
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: ﻿For some time after its birth, it uses the nutrients that are packed into the seed on its back in order to grow.

Name: Ivysaur
Flavor-Text: The more sunlight Ivysaur bathes in, the more strength wells up within it, allowing the bud on its back to grow larger.

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
This attack does 30 more damage for each [G] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is called the Walking Jungle. Each of the nuts has a face and a will of its own.

Name: Shuckle
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon has any [G] Energy attached, you may use this Ability. Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The berries stored in its vaselike shell eventually become a thick, pulpy juice.

Name: Ninjask
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for a Shedinja and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is so quick, it is said to be able to avoid any attack. It loves to feed on tree sap.

Name: Vulpix
Flavor-Text: If it is attacked by an enemy that is stronger than itself, it feigns injury to fool the enemy and escapes.

Name: Litleo
Flavor-Text: When Litleo are young, female Pyroar will teach them how to hunt. Once the Litleo mature, they will leave the pride and set out on their own.

Name: Snover
Flavor-Text: During cold seasons, it migrates to the mountain’s lower reaches. It returns to the snow-covered summit in the spring.

Name: Clawitzer
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from the Active Spot to your Bench, you may use this Ability. Attach up to 2 Basic [W] Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Clawitzer can move through the water at a speed of 60 knots by using gas inside its body to expel water from the back of its claw.

Name: Inteleon
Effects: Choose a Pokémon in play (yours or your opponent’s) that has the least HP remaining, except for this Pokémon, and it is Knocked Out.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses special lenses in its eyes to sense things about its opponents—like their body heat—then attacks once it identifies a weak spot.

Name: Helioptile
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The sun powers this Pokémon’s electricity generation. Interruption of that process stresses Helioptile to the point of weakness.

Name: Shedinja
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon ex, your opponent can’t take any Prize cards for it.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A strange Pokémon—it flies without moving its wings, has a hollow shell for a body, and does not breathe.

Name: Houndstone
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into their deck.
Flavor-Text: Houndstone spends most of its time sleeping in graveyards. Among all the dog Pokémon, this one is most loyal to its master.

Name: Marshadow
Effects: If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can conceal itself in any shadow, so it went undiscovered for a long time.

Name: Garganacl
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by your [F] Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Garganacl will rub its fingertips together and sprinkle injured Pokémon with salt. Even severe wounds will promptly heal afterward.

Name: Spiritomb
Ability-Effect: If your Active [D] Pokémon is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if your Active [D] Pokémon is Knocked Out), place 1 damage counter on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.

Name: Shroodle
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Though usually a mellow Pokémon, it will sink its sharp, poison-soaked front teeth into any that anger it, causing paralysis in the object of its ire.

Name: Steelix
Effects: If you have exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, this attack does 200 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It is thought its body transformed as a result of iron accumulating internally from swallowing soil.

Name: Spearow
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Due to its short wings, it can’t fly long distances. It wanders about restlessly and pecks at bug Pokémon.

Name: Delibird
Effects: If you go first, you can use this attack during your first turn. Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It carries food all day long. There are tales about lost people who were saved by the food it had.

Name: Gumshoos
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Switch a card from your hand with the top card of your deck.
Flavor-Text: Once it finds signs of prey, it will patiently stake out the location, waiting until the sun goes down.

Name: Stufful
Flavor-Text: The way it protects itself by flailing its arms may be an adorable sight, but stay well away. This is flailing that can snap thick tree trunks.

Name: Mega Venusaur ex
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may use this Ability. Move a Basic [G] Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Camerupt ex
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Burned, this attack does 160 more damage.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Abomasnow ex
Effects: Discard the top 6 cards of your deck, and this attack does 100 damage for each Basic [W] Energy card that you discarded in this way.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Manectric ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.
You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon and have this attack do 130 more damage.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Gardevoir ex
Effects: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a Basic [P] Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 50 damage for each [P] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Lucario ex
Effects: Attach up to 3 Basic [F] Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Mega Brave.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Absol ex
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has exactly 6 damage counters on it, that Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Your opponent reveals their hand, and you discard a card you find there.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Mawile ex
Effects: This attack does 80 damage for each Prize card you have taken.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack’s base damage is 30.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Latias ex
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Kangaskhan ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may use this Ability. Draw 2 cards. You can’t use more than 1 Run Errand Ability each turn.
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Acerola’s Mischief
Description: You can use this card only if your opponent has 2 or fewer Prize cards remaining.Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play. During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to that Pokémon by your opponent’s Pokémon ex.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Air Balloon
Description: The Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is [C][C] less.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Buddy-Buddy Poffin
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon with 70 HP or less and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Fighting Gong
Description: Search your deck for a Basic [F] Energy card or a Basic [F] Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Lillie’s Determination
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards. If you have exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, draw 8 cards instead.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Lt. Surge’s Bargain
Description: Ask your opponent if each player may take a Prize card. If yes, each player takes a Prize card. If no, you draw 4 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Mega Signal
Description: Search your deck for a Mega Evolution Pokémon ex, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Mystery Garden
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may discard an Energy card from their hand in order to draw cards until they have as many cards in their hand as they have [P] Pokémon in play.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Night Stretcher
Description: Put a Pokémon or a Basic Energy card from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Premium Power Pro
Description: During this turn, attacks used by your [F] Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
